items.headline: CAPE System Launch Expected April 20 as CBP Completes Final Testing
items.summary: Phase 1 of CBP's CAPE system is expected to launch by April 20, 2026, with CBP targeting that date for accepting refund claims. The claim portal is 85% complete and in testing, while development continues on other components. Only importers with complete ACE and ACH enrollment will be able to submit claims when the portal opens.

items.headline: CBP Reports 45-Day Processing Timeline for Accepted CAPE Claims
items.summary: CBP has stated it will process accepted refund requests within 45 days from the date of acceptance, unless there is a compliance concern requiring further review. CBP estimates Phase 1 functionality will process approximately 63% of entries for which IEEPA tariff refunds are due. The system will handle unliquidated entries and those within the 90-day voluntary reliquidation period first.

items.headline: 92% of Eligible Importers Still Not Enrolled for Electronic Refunds
items.summary: More than 26,000 importers have enrolled in ACH refunds, representing 78% of impacted entries and $120 billion in duty value, but this leaves 92% of the 330,000 eligible importers still not enrolled. As of February 6, 2026, CBP requires all duty refunds to be issued via electronic funds transfer, meaning non-enrolled importers will face delays.

items.headline: Court Confirms CBP Making 'Satisfactory Progress' on CAPE Development
items.summary: On April 1, the Court of International Trade issued an order confirming that CBP is making 'satisfactory progress' on all four core CAPE components and reaffirming that refunds will be issued electronically. As of March 30, CBP estimates the claim portal is 85% complete, mass processing 60% complete, review and liquidation 80% complete, and refund 75% complete.

items.headline: CBP Confirms Finally Liquidated Entries Excluded from Phase 1
items.summary: CBP has committed to developing capability to process claims for finally liquidated entries in a subsequent phase, but has not provided a timeframe for doing so. On March 27, the CIT ordered that finally liquidated entries shall be reliquidated without regard to IEEPA duties, but CBP will exclude them from Phase 1 to keep on schedule.

items.headline: Court Expands Refund Order to Include Finally Liquidated Entries
items.summary: On March 27, the Court of International Trade issued an amended order requiring all final and liquidated entries (where the 180-day protest period has expired) to be reliquidated without regard to IEEPA duties. This significantly expands the scope of required refunds beyond CBP's initial Phase 1 capabilities.

items.headline: Government Appeal Deadline Approaches in Early May 2026
items.summary: The government has until approximately early May 2026 to appeal the CIT's March 4 order directing IEEPA refunds. The government has signaled it will challenge the CIT's order and may seek to stay the refund directive at the Federal Circuit Court of Appeals. This remains a key development to monitor for timing of actual refunds.
